summary: Mila came to class in a very happy mood. She knew some vocabulary and could repeat the structures too. She needed help with the "taking off" and "putting on" words during the weather routines. She got upset because she could not see the Big Book during story time, but did not move to be able to see. She laid down a few times, was asked to sit, and turned her back to me during the whole story because she was angry. She could do the action activity quite well and was happy to participate.

The Oliver Thursday students were all great and participated in the activities very well. Mila did close up a bit when she did not get her way, but it got better. They all loved the present activity, all wanted to help hand out the material, and they all got a green face.

Mila only listened at first. She was a bit upset because she couldn’t do the stamp and then did not take part in the routines. She then said she had a headache and did take part in the activities. The class could tell who was missing and name the family member, repeat the correct structure each time, pair up the correct sentence with the weather realia item, and say most of the words, although they sometimes needed a bit of help. They needed to move a lot and seemed to get bored by the end of the story.

Mila could tell the story with almost no help. She got upset by the end of class because I did not allow her to keep the blu-tack. She refused to take part in the True or False activity and the Dice Game.
Mila came today. She knew most of the vocabulary, structures, songs, gestures, and story. They all loved A present for me, Dice Game, and Family Bingo.

Mila could combine the mood and the actions very well. She knew the phrase that went with her weather card, and she knew the story quite well. She could already tell part of it and repeat the rest. They learned how to say what they were wearing, and they learned the color white and the color black today. They could do the weather and clothing item combination very well, and they did the worksheet activity very, very well. They also showed that they knew the color vocabulary, which was great. All of them could do the color vocabulary, and I was really proud of them, and their behavior was relatively great today, so I was really happy with them.
Mila already knew the structures and understood how to use them, so she could repeat them very well. She could also use the new vocabulary words and understood how to use them during the activities.
